behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between prompt/request construction and the API client call. A pure unit test of a helper string function can miss wiring bugs where the final payload omits or truncates labels.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier with a fake API transport that records outbound requests. Call classify() and assert the captured payload contains every configured taxonomy label exactly once in the instruction/context sent to the API.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The bug appears at the module boundary where external response data is validated and mapped before returning to callers. Mocking only internal mapping logic as a unit can miss response-shape/adapter wiring issues.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client returning an out-of-taxonomy label (for example "medical"). Execute classify() through the public module entrypoint and assert the returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output stability. A mocked model is deterministic and would hide nondeterminism or sampling/config drift that only appears with real LLM calls.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real API classifications for the same document under production inference settings (same model and parameters) and assert labels are stable across runs within an allowed tolerance policy (for strict determinism, all runs equal). Fail if variance appears.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This is a real-cost boundary tied to external API token accounting. Mock usage values can always pass and hide provider-side counting differences from local estimates.
behaviors.test_description: Send a representative document to the real API and read actual usage tokens returned by the provider. Assert total consumed tokens for the call are <= configured budget; include the full production prompt/template path in the test.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The key failure is retry-policy wiring: interpreting rate-limit errors, waiting/backoff, and retrying through the client. This is testable with controlled fakes; real network is not required for minimum failure detection.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client that returns a rate-limit error on first call and success on second, plus a controllable clock/sleeper. Assert classify() eventually returns the successful label, performed two calls, and invoked delay/backoff before retry.
